cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Crunchbase CRO on the real obstacle to AI-driven ROI: data quality, not models
stories.plain: Crunchbase's chief revenue officer argues that the bottleneck to proving AI value in go-to-market work isn't the model — it's the quality of data being fed in. This reframes how marketing leaders should prioritise investment: fixing data foundations before scaling AI tooling, a practical lesson for anyone being asked to justify AI spend to a CFO.

stories.head: Multiply's '10 Minute ABM' compresses 100 hours of personalised ad creation into minutes using self-learning AI
stories.plain: Multiply's new tool lets B2B marketers auto-generate hundreds of 1:1 personalised ads for target accounts in roughly ten minutes, replacing work that previously took days. It also applies self-learning optimisation to improve which ads win over time — combining generative creation with bandit-style feedback loops, directly changing how ABM campaigns are built and bought.

stories.head: Pega's Don Schuerman: most companies are applying AI to the wrong processes
stories.plain: Pega's chief technology officer explains why the wave of AI pilots is failing to produce predictable results: enterprises are layering AI onto outdated processes instead of redesigning those processes for autonomous decision-making. The piece gives marketing leaders a practical framework for agentic AI deployment, covering governance and where human oversight must remain.

stories.head: ZoomInfo launches open-source GTM.AI CLI giving AI agents direct access to verified B2B data
stories.plain: ZoomInfo's new open-source command-line tool lets developers and AI agents query verified go-to-market data directly from a terminal or automated pipeline — no GUI required. This changes how agentic sales and marketing systems are built: verified firmographic data can now be pulled programmatically into agent workflows, lowering the barrier to building data-grounded GTM automation.

stories.head: Jonah rolls out schema architecture to make multifamily property sites visible inside AI search answers
stories.plain: Jonah, a website platform for apartment communities, has embedded Schema.org structured data across all its premium sites so AI search engines like Perplexity and Google's AI Overviews surface property information accurately. This is a concrete vertical-market example of GEO in production, showing how structured data strategy is becoming a standard feature of website platforms — not an afterthought.

stories.head: The CMS is becoming the AI operating system for brands
stories.plain: This piece argues that choosing a CMS in 2026 is really a decision about your AI infrastructure — covering AI discovery, governance, personalization, and agentic workflows all at once. It matters because it reframes a tool most marketers treat as a publishing utility into the central control layer for how AI finds, shapes, and delivers brand content.

stories.head: How AI discovery is changing everything marketers measure
stories.plain: AI-powered answers are hollowing out click-through traffic, making traditional metrics like sessions and impressions misleading. The article lays out what to measure instead — a practical shift that affects every marketing budget conversation and every attribution model currently in use.

stories.head: Expert Mode: The Uncomfortable Truth Between AI Ambition and AI Reality with Pega's Don Schuerman
stories.plain: Pega's CTO makes the case that most AI pilots fail not because the models are weak but because underlying processes were built for a pre-AI era. This is a direct challenge to how marketing leaders are currently justifying AI spend, and it reframes what 'AI readiness' actually requires before buying more tools.

stories.head: Multiply Launches '10 Minute ABM,' enabling 100s of personalized 1:1 ads for top accounts in minutes
stories.plain: Multiply's tool compresses account-based ad creative from days of work into minutes using self-learning, personalized ad generation at scale. It matters because it shows agentic AI moving into paid media buying and creative production simultaneously — changing how ABM budgets and workflows are structured.

stories.head: HubSpot's May 2026 updates: Breeze gets serious, automation gets smarter, and your data gets cleaner
stories.plain: HubSpot's Breeze AI is evolving from a prompt-response assistant into an active participant in marketing workflows — triggering automations, cleaning data, and making decisions without waiting for a human prompt. Because HubSpot sits in millions of SMB stacks, these changes set a new baseline expectation for what a CEP should do autonomously.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: AI Search & Trust as Visibility Currency
body: As buyers increasingly discover products through AI-powered search rather than traditional SERPs, the rules of visibility are rewriting themselves. Being cited by an AI answer engine now depends on structured data, authoritative signals, and schema architecture — not keyword density alone.

Jonah's rollout of specialised schema for property sites and the broader push for 'AI visibility strategies' signal that marketers must treat trust infrastructure as a first-order SEO decision. How AI discovery changes what you measure — impressions, citations, referral path — is a decision-level shift, not a tactical tweak.
ev: MarTech Series · schema for AI visibility

themes.head: AI ROI Reckoning: Proving Value Before Scaling
body: The honeymoon phase of AI investment is ending. Executives are now demanding proof of return before authorising further spend, and the gap between AI ambition and AI reality is becoming a boardroom conversation, not just a practitioner complaint.

Crunchbase's CRO and Pega's Don Schuerman both point to the same trap: teams are using AI to scale the wrong parts of go-to-market — amplifying content volume and outreach while neglecting the conversion-quality leaks driving CAC upward. The decision that changes is where you deploy automation first.
ev: Agile Brand · Crunchbase CRO on AI ROI
Agile Brand · AI ambition vs reality, Pega
MarTech · scaling the wrong GTM parts

themes.head: The CMS & Stack Become the AI Operating Layer
body: Content management systems are evolving from publishing tools into the central orchestration layer for AI-driven brand operations — controlling data flows, agent triggers, and personalisation logic in one place. This reframes the CMS purchase from a content decision to an infrastructure decision.

At the same time, fragmented stacks are being exposed as a hidden tax: redundant tools drain budget and create data silos that prevent AI from working coherently. HubSpot's Breeze updates and the Globant-Vercel 'AI Pods' alliance both push toward tighter, unified architectures rather than more vendors.
ev: MarTech · hidden cost of fragmented stack

themes.head: Agentic Workflows Move from Demo to Deployment
body: AI agents are crossing from proof-of-concept into production-grade marketing workflows — handling tasks like campaign briefs, data enrichment, and ABM ad creation with minimal human intervention. Multiply's '10 Minute ABM' and the Hermes Agent Desktop both represent real shipped tooling, not future roadmap promises.

The strategic shift is that agentic systems change who owns a workflow: a junior analyst with the right agent can now execute work that previously required a specialist team. ZoomInfo's GTM.AI CLI bringing verified data to the command line is another signal that agents are being built into the developer & ops layer, not just the marketer-facing UI.

themes.head: Data Quality & GTM Alignment as AI's Real Bottleneck
body: Across the month, a consistent diagnosis emerged: AI amplifies bad data and misaligned go-to-market motion faster than it fixes them. Rising customer acquisition costs while deal quality stagnates is the symptom; dirty CRM data and siloed GTM teams are the cause.

Qualtrics betting its product roadmap on data as 'the edge in AI-driven experience management' and Genpact's global capability centre with Nestlé both reflect the same conclusion: before AI can personalise or predict, the data foundation must be trusted and unified. This makes data governance a prerequisite investment, not an afterthought.

month.takeaway: The month's signal is unified: AI's limiting factor is no longer capability but the quality of data, stack coherence, and honest ROI accounting underneath it — teams that fix the foundation first will compound their AI advantage while others simply scale their existing mistakes.
